summary: First-pass full backend + smoke frontend test for the L'Oreal GenAI Observability dashboard. 32/35 pytest tests pass (91%). Login, JWT auth, filters, overview KPIs, traces (list+detail), logs, cost (3 group_bys), sessions, tools, search, top-traces, models, errors, pricing CRUD, meta last-refresh, refresh pipeline mock all work and return populated 30-day seed data. Frontend login (admin1/pwd1) succeeds, dashboard renders with non-zero KPIs ($0.0123 cost, 16 traces, 70 spans, 50,432 tokens, P50=2152.9ms, P95=3928.7ms) and FilterBar/tabs/charts. 3 backend bugs surfaced and are user-visible in the UI (Metrics tab + Health tab).

critical.issue: Two routes are registered at the same path: line 212 (public, returns {status: 'ok'}) and line 730 (authenticated, returns list of service-health rows). FastAPI resolves the FIRST one, so the second authenticated handler is dead code. Frontend Health tab calls /api/health expecting an array and crashes with React error 'rawData.some is not a function'. Fix: rename the authenticated route to something like /api/health/services (and update frontend) OR remove the public /api/health and let the authenticated one win (but then unauthenticated health probe breaks).

Returns 422 'Field required _kw'. The signature uses `**_kw` which FastAPI interprets as a required query parameter named '_kw' (Pydantic v2). Also the endpoint is MISSING `_user=Depends(require_auth)` so it would be unprotected if fixed. Fix: remove **_kw and add the auth dependency.
Same bug as above — `**_kw` causes 422 and there is no auth dependency. Breaks the Metrics tab table.

minor.issue: kpis.peak_instances / peak_cpu_pct / peak_mem_pct are not returned by /api/overview at all (only by /api/metrics/summary). UI Overview tile shows 0/0/0 for infra metrics — this matches the agent-to-agent note (no GCP infra data), but the keys are absent from /api/overview's KPI payload. Not blocking.

Some time-range presets (5m/10m/30m/1h) will frequently produce empty results because the seed data is sparse at minute granularity. The default UI range is 'Last 1 day' which is fine, but a user picking 'Last 5m' on a fresh server will see empty charts — expected given seed.

The latency query uses `{w} AND parent_span_id IS NULL ORDER BY ...`.replace('WHERE','WHERE',1) — the .replace is a no-op (replaces WHERE with itself). Confusing dead code, should be removed for clarity. Functionally OK because `build_scope` already returns 'WHERE ...'.

ui_bugs.issue: Crashes with 'rawData.some is not a function' when /api/health returns {status:'ok'} instead of an array (root cause: duplicate backend route — see backend critical #1). Caught via React error boundary in antd Table.
Calls /api/metrics/timeseries and /api/metrics; both return 422, so charts and tables on the Metrics tab will be empty. Root cause: backend critical #2 + #3.

The 'Sign in with Google' button is shown even though /api/config returns google_client_id=''. Clicking it pops a toast 'Google sign-in unavailable here'. Consider hiding the button when no client id is configured (test_credentials.md states it should already be hidden).

integration_issues.issue: Frontend expects array of service rows; backend returns {status:'ok'}. React render crashes.
All metrics endpoints return 422 due to **_kw signature bug

action_items: Fix duplicate @app.get('/api/health'): rename the authenticated service-health route (e.g. /api/health/services) and update frontend Health view to call it.
Remove the `**_kw` parameter from /api/metrics/timeseries and /api/metrics; add `_user=Depends(require_auth)` to both for security parity with other endpoints.
Optionally hide 'Sign in with Google' button when GET /api/config returns empty google_client_id (cleanup).
Clean up dead `.replace('WHERE','WHERE',1)` in /api/overview latency query.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py has duplicate route paths (/api/health) — silent shadowing bug.
Two endpoints use `**_kw` in signature which Pydantic v2 treats as a required query field; this is a FastAPI footgun.
Inconsistent auth: /api/metrics/timeseries and /api/metrics are missing `Depends(require_auth)` while every other /api/* endpoint enforces it — security inconsistency.
server.py is 787 lines and mixes DB schema, helpers, auth, and 30+ endpoints — should be split into modules (routes_*.py, db.py, auth.py) for maintainability (per code-review guideline >700 lines).
Many places use inline `if x: ...; pp.append(...)` (semicolon multi-statement) which violates E701/E702 lint rules — readability hit (Python lint reports 30+ such occurrences).
MongoDB is not used here (SQLite), so no _id concern — good.

success_rate.frontend: 85% (login + 6/7 tabs + KPIs OK; Health & Metrics broken due to backend bugs)

seed_data_creation: Backend bootstrap_db() seeded ~2000+ rows across 17 wide_* tables for last 30 days on first run. Pricing test inserts a TEST_model_x row that is not cleaned up — minor.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests in /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py can be re-run with `pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py -v`. The 3 failures (test_metrics_timeseries, test_metrics_table, test_health_services_with_token) are all rooted in 2 code bugs (duplicate /api/health route + **_kw in two endpoints). Frontend Health and Metrics tabs will start working once these are fixed. Other tabs (Overview, Traces, Logs, LLM Cost, Sessions, Tool Calls, Insights, Compare) render correctly with seed data. Default UI time range is 'Last 1 day' which already shows populated data.
rca of the issue: 1) /api/health duplicate: server.py defines @app.get('/api/health') twice (line 212 public, line 730 authenticated). FastAPI registers both but matches by registration order — first wins. Frontend Health.tsx assumes it gets the authenticated list response, leading to runtime React crash 'rawData.some is not a function'. Reproduce: login -> click Health tab -> see error in console. Mitigation: rename second handler to /api/health/services and update frontend call. 2) /api/metrics/timeseries 422: signature `def metrics_ts(..., **_kw)`. In Pydantic v2 + FastAPI, **kwargs is treated as required query field. Reproduce: `curl -H 'Authorization: Bearer X' '/api/metrics/timeseries?time_range=30d'` returns 422 with `loc:['query','_kw'] msg:'Field required'`. Mitigation: drop **_kw, add explicit parameters, add `_user=Depends(require_auth)`. Same fix for /api/metrics.
